titleStmt.title: SII 9.154: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 154. (A.R. No. 233 of 1918.) ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE RUINED BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT RAGIMASALAVADA, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is damaged and the closing portion is lost. It is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana (= A.D. 1085-86), the rest being lost. It mentions the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and his feudatory Tribhuvanamalla-Pāṇḍyadēva, who was ruling over Noḷambavāḍi thirtytwo-thousand and Gaṅgavāḍi ninetysix-thousand.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
namaścyaṁdralalāṭākṣasi(ṁ)ndhujāsyēṁdudarṣanē . jagajjananakarttutvalīlāmātrāya śaṁbhavē .
samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirājaṁ para
mēśvaraṁ paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrī
matribhuvanamalladēvaru . v® . Abhayamenalke takka paracakranripa
neyde kādu satṟubhaṭanriparkkaḷaṁ yamabhaṭaṁge nivēdisi tanna kīrttiyaṁ na
maniḷātaḷāntyamenajāṇḍamaneydisi bīradēḷgeyiṁ tṛbhuvanamallacakri
rttisidaṁ bhuvanatrayaṁgaḷa . ka . subhaṭanṛpamakuṭamāṇikyabhāsu
ḷagōpaḷanakharuciyaṁ prabhe mikku tējadindaṁ tṛbhuvanamallāṁ
ka nirmmaḷeyādaḷu . va . Antenisi negaḻda tribhuvanamalladēvara vijaya
rājyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire ta
tpādapadmōpajīvi samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara ku
ḷubapuravarēsvaraṁ yaduvaṁśāmbaradyumaṇi sūtracūḍāma
ṇi nijakuḷakamaḷamārttaṇḍaṁ paricchēdigaṇḍaṁ rājigacōḷamanō
bhaṁgaṁ śrīmattribhuvanamallapādābjabhṛṁgaṁ śrīmanmahāma
ṇḍaḷēsvaraṁ tṛbhuvanamallapāṇḍyadēvaru . ka . rājigacōḷadvipamṛgarājaṁ śrī
pāṇḍyarājanūrjjitatējaṁ vidviṣṭārātige mige jōḍenisi nūḷaṁbavāḍiyaṁ
da . va . Antenisi tṛbhu dēvaru noḷaṁbavāḍi mūva
gaṁgavāḍi toṁbhatta nigrahasiṣṭapra
geyyuttamirddu
mmigoṭṭiya bīḍina varada
kālada 10 neya krōdhanasaṁvatsarada
tiyapātadandu
caṭa The stone is broken after this.
Digital edition of SII 9.154 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
